Hyperbilirubinemia was diagnosed neonatally. Mild aortic arch hypoplasia/mild coarctation of the aorta, and patent ductus arteriosus, both of the latter resolved by age 1. Gastric volvulus was identified at 4 months of life which required surgical repair. Left ventricular non-compaction cardiomyopathy with characteristics of LV dysfunction and persistent apical trabeculations resolved by age 5. Speech articulation disorder, delay in expressive language, gastroesophageal reflux, and failure to thrive. Fine kinetic hand tremor. Abnormal neuroimaging: Brain parenchyma was notable for T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the periorlandic white matter extending to the posterior limb of the internal capsule and posterior periventricular white matter. Family history was significant for a brother with autism spectrum disorder, postaxial polydactyly type B present in both hands and left foot, and tall stature.
